wonder if this would work inside FF’s helmets??? INSTAGRAM tech
Love this breakdown! 🔥  That green sticker in the NFL word is called the green dot. You may watch games and see an announcer refer to it like “____ is wearing the green dot.” Usually the green dot is worn by a LB or Safety on Defense, then the QB is always wearing the green dot on offense.  Another interesting thing about the headsets cutting out at 15 seconds: many times a QB doesn’t realize it. It sounds weird. One second someone is talking to you then it’s crickets. Then, you either need to call your own play as a QB or if you could piece together what the coach was saying before it cut out, then you can just say the play.  Would love more of these sports tech breakdowns! 🔥 INSTAGRAM tech

¿QUÉ PASÓ CON LA DIVINIDAD DE CRISTO EN LA CRUZ? Leamos las siguientes declaraciones de Ellen White. «Cuando Cristo fue crucificado, su naturaleza humana fue la que murió. La Deidad no disminuyó ni murió; esto habría sido imposible» (Manuscript Releases [Manuscritos publicados], t. 21, p. 418). «"Yo soy la resurrección y la vida" (Juan 11: 25). El que había dicho: "Yo pongo mi vida, para volverla a tomar" (Juan 10: 17), salió de la tumba por la vida que estaba en él mismo. Murió la humanidad, no murió la divinidad. En su divinidad, Cristo poseía el poder de romper las ataduras de la muerte. Declara que tiene vida en sí mismo para resucitar a quien quiera» (Mensajes selectos, t.1, p. 354). "La Deidad no murió. La naturaleza humana del Hijo de María, ¿fue cambiada en la naturaleza divina del Hijo de Dios? No. Las dos naturalezas se mezclaron misteriosamente en una sola persona: el hombre Cristo Jesús. En él moraba toda la plenitud de la Deidad corporalmente. Cuando Cristo fue crucificado, su naturaleza humana fue la que murió. La Deidad no disminuyó y murió; esto habría sido imposible. Cristo, el inmaculado, salvará a cada hijo e hija de Adán que acepte la salvación que se le ofrece, que consienta en convertirse en hijo o hija de Dios. El Salvador ha comprado a la raza caída con su propia sangre. Este es un gran misterio, un misterio que no será comprendido plena y completamente, en toda su grandeza, hasta que los redimidos sean trasladados. Entonces se comprenderán el poder, la grandeza y la eficacia de la dádiva de Dios para el hombre. Pero el enemigo ha decidido que esta dádiva sea oscurecida hasta el punto de que quede reducida a nada (Carta 280, 1904) [Comentario bíblico adventista, t.5, p. 1088]. «Yo soy la resurrección y la vida». El que había dicho: «Pongo mi vida, para volverla a tomar» salió de la tumba por la vida que estaba en él mismo. La humanidad murió: la divinidad no murió. Por su divinidad Cristo tenía poder para romper las ataduras de la muerte. Él declara que tiene vida en sí mismo para dar vida a quienes les plazca.Todos los seres creados viven por la voluntad y el poder de Dios. Son recipientes de la vida del Hijo de Dios. @sustidwell Happy to expand! Think of emotion regulation as a two-way street: "top-down" and "bottom-up."  Top-down relies on your prefrontal cortex (logic, journaling, cognitive behavioral tools). Bottom-up relies on your physiology (heart rate, breathing, cortisol, HPA axis).  When your autonomic nervous system is exhausted and stuck in a chronic stress response, the survival part of your brain literally shuts down the prefrontal cortex to conserve energy. That’s why you can know exactly how you should react in a situation, but you still lose your temper or shut down. Your biology hijacked your psychology.  To get your "power steering" back, you have to send safety signals from the bottom-up first. For example: 1️⃣ Using extended exhales (like the physiological sigh) to manually increase vagal tone. 2️⃣ Stabilizing blood sugar so your brain doesn't interpret an energy crash as a survival threat.  You can't journal your way out of a physiological deficit. You have to figure out if your bottleneck is Neural, Metabolic, or Regulatory. INSTAGRAM education

This is a brilliant distinction. Over-analyzing our emotions can actually trap us in a hyper-vigilant state rather than helping us process them. 🎯  What so many driven people miss is that emotional regulation isn't just a psychological skill—it’s a physiological capacity. When your autonomic nervous system is pushed into Regulatory Overload (low vagal tone, disrupted cortisol rhythm), your baseline emotional stability physically plummets. You simply lose the biological bandwidth for high EQ.  Trying to "think" your way out of an emotional spiral when your HPA axis is dysregulated is like trying to steer a car without power steering. You cannot out-think a physiological bottleneck. Restoring the autonomic rhythm has to come first before top-down psychological tools can actually work. Such a vital conversation! 🧠⚡️ INSTAGRAM education

Emotional intelligence is often misunderstood as constant monitoring, which in reality only increases systemic noise. Brackett’s approach of 'saying hello' to an emotion is a masterclass in efficient signal processing.  In my architecture, we view emotional spikes as system interrupts. If you try to debug every feeling in real-time, you drain your cognitive load and accelerate HPA-axis technical debt. Acknowledging the signal without over-processing it is the most efficient way to maintain structural integrity.  This is the core of the Bio-Mechanical Choice: choosing stability over fragmentation. Precision regulation at its finest. 🏛️ INSTAGRAM education

What’s fascinating here is the idea that the ego and consciousness, while linked, don’t have to be fused. The ego is the survival lens; it filters experience through “me vs. the world.” But consciousness, when expanded through meditation, flow, or empathy, shows us that awareness is bigger than that filter. It’s like stepping outside the box you didn’t even realize you were in.  The power of these practices isn’t just spiritual, it’s neurological. Brain circuits governing self-referential thought quiet down, while networks for attention, creativity, and connection light up. In those moments, you’re not trapped in rumination; you’re rewiring your brain to experience life with more clarity, curiosity, and compassion.  The takeaway? The ego isn’t the enemy, it’s just a tool. But when you learn how to uncouple it from consciousness, you stop mistaking the tool for the whole self and that’s where real freedom begins. Awe is a real and fascinating emotional state, but this is also where a meaningful effect can get overextended into a broad health promise. Feeling awe may reduce stress and shift attention away from the self, yes. But potential benefits for pain, wellbeing, or even longevity do not mean awe functions like a direct intervention with predictable clinical outcomes. That distinction matters. An interesting psychological state is not the same as a standardized health tool. The science gets much stronger when we separate subjective experience, plausible mechanism, and actual long term outcome data. Still, this is a valuable topic because it points to something modern life often strips away. Not more stimulation, but more perspective. If people want to understand where neuroscience ends and overclaiming begins, that is exactly what we break down on our profile. INSTAGRAM education

Habits don’t form with motivation — they form with repetition that rewires neurons. Every time you repeat an action, dopamine marks it as valuable and myelin strengthens the neural circuit. Within weeks, the brain shifts effort from the prefrontal cortex (willpower) to the basal ganglia (automation). The trick? Pair the habit with a cue and immediate reward — emotion locks memory faster than logic. At @metamorphosis3393, we decode how neuroplasticity, focus, and discipline connect from first principles. It’s a myth that “fasted training doesn’t work for women.” There are four studies with female participants demonstrating that fasted training results in similar changes in fat loss and muscle growth or changes in lean mass.  PMID: 23723099 PMID: 25429252 PMID: 40335157 PMID: 30366741  If you’re performing a long/intense exercise session, fueling first might help with performance. Ultimately the decision to train fed or fasted should be based on individual preference, regardless of sex. INSTAGRAM education

What’s powerful here is the psychology behind stacking small wins. Every time you check off a daily goal, you’re not just crossing a task off a list; you’re teaching your brain that you’re the kind of person who follows through. That identity shift compounds, and suddenly goals that once felt out of reach start to feel inevitable.  I’ve seen this with clients who begin with something as simple as journaling their meals or hitting three workouts a week. Within months, their vision of what’s possible for their health expands, not because the mountain got smaller, but because their self-trust got stronger.  It’s a reminder that consistency isn’t just about progress; it’s about rewiring belief. And that’s what ultimately makes massive goals achievable. INSTAGRAM education

@adam_greis yaaaa OK lmfao INSTAGRAM education
The largest and most current evidence shows no statistically significant link between cannabis use and lower testosterone or broad sperm damage in the general population. A 2021 meta-analysis of over 4,000 men found no significant difference in the risk of abnormal semen parameters (P = 0.369) or in testosterone levels (P = 0.318). The most recent and robust evidence, a 2025 preconception cohort study (PRESTO) of 921 men, concluded that "current cannabis use was not appreciably associated with semen quality". While a 2026 study on 2,074 infertile men did find lower testosterone (P = 0.03) and worse sperm morphology (P = 0.007) in users, this specific finding applies only to that high-risk subgroup. Therefore, for the average man, the highest-quality data suggests that any potential effect on testosterone or overall sperm health is not statistically detectable. INSTAGRAM education

Bob Marley INSTAGRAM education
A Claude AI deep dive into the studies informing this claim just as a FYI: Bottom Line The evidence that heavy, chronic cannabis use harms sperm quality (particularly morphology, motility, and DNA integrity) is moderately convincing, with a plausible mechanism. But for occasional or moderate use, the picture is genuinely unclear — some studies even show a paradoxical benefit in sperm count. The data is weakened by reliance on self-reporting, small samples, confounding variables, and heavy use of animal models. This is an area where the science is real but far from settled INSTAGRAM education

"Recent studies (2020–2025) indicate that cannabis compounds, particularly cannabidiol (CBD) and cannabigerol (CBG), possess potent antimicrobial properties against a variety of pathogens, notably Gram-positive bacteria, drug-resistant strains, and certain dental plaque pathogens. These findings position cannabinoids as potential novel, non-traditional agents to fight antimicrobial resistance." Source references : 1. https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11858408/ 2. https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9607911/#:~:text=The%20development%20of%20new%20antibiotics,future%20perspectives%20in%20biomedical%20application. 3. https://www.mdpi.com/2673-9879/4/3/33#:~:text=Abstract,effective%20in%20broad%2Dspectrum%20therapies. 👩‍🔬🙋‍♀️PhD Cannabis pharmacologist here - as researchers we can NOT keep categorizing “cannabis” as ONE thing. It is NOT one thing.It is 500 different bioactive molecules each with unique pharmacology. Assuming this clip is referring to high THC cannabis use, CBD and other cannabis molecules work completely differently. Also,do we have RCTs to confirm this is actually the cannabinoid use and not other variable in the group like other substance use or socioeconomic factors or something? If we dont start differentiating between molecules in the cannabis plant when we make statements ,we only perpetuate confusion. Do better. INSTAGRAM education

@hubermanlab @nataliecrawfordmd There’s likely a signal here, especially with higher THC and frequent use but framing cannabis as the primary cause in most cases feels like an oversimplification of a very multifactorial process.  In clinic, sleep, alcohol, metabolic health, and stress are often just as important.  It would be helpful to move away from these black-and-white narratives “all good” or “all bad.” There’s a meaningful difference between high-potency, chronic use (usually smoking) and low-dose, oral cannabinoid use (CBD/CBG or small amounts of THC) for sleep.  The nuance around dose, pattern, and context is where this becomes clinically useful and where physicians should be leading the conversation. INSTAGRAM education
In studies done in rats feeding them shelf stable processed flour versus freshly milled flour with full germ, bran and endosperm present, within 4 generations the processed flour rats were infertile but the freshly milled flour fed rats were not. They equivalate that to 100 years in humans, which we started removing the germ and the bran from flour to keep it shelf stable in the early 1900s. Fascinating and terrifying. It makes me wonder about all of the inflammation and hormone issues in women specifically, like, does that contribute to PCOS or Endometriosis? INSTAGRAM education
A systematic review I read from 2019 say that the studies suggesting this are mostly animal models and is have not been replicated in humans- where the research is more inconclusive. That it has some impact I’m sure, but this might also be due to neurobiological changes that frequent use can cause. There’s also one study in hum reprod from Nassan et al 2019 that found marijuana smoking was associated with higher sperm concentrations than non-smokers. It can make you very horny too, so I’m sure the truth is more nuanced than she says. INSTAGRAM education

Good thing I got neutered INSTAGRAM education
This is an important reminder that fertility is not just reproductive… it’s a metabolic and cellular health signal.  Sperm quality reflects oxidative stress, mitochondrial function, hormone balance, and even lifestyle patterns over the past ~90 days. Cannabis may be one lever, but the deeper picture is the environment those cells are developing in.  What moves the needle most consistently: Stable blood sugar, adequate protein and micronutrients (zinc, selenium, omega-3s), quality sleep, and resistance training to support testosterone and insulin sensitivity.  And here’s the part many overlook… Heat, toxins, and recovery matter. Chronic sauna exposure without recovery, tight clothing, poor sleep, and endocrine disruptors all compound the issue. The DNA fragmentation piece is what I wish more people understood — it's not just "can you conceive," it's the quality of what gets passed on. I've had patients go through multiple failed IVF cycles with no clear female-factor explanation, and when we finally dug into lifestyle — cannabis, heat exposure, chronic stress — the picture changed. The 90-day spermatogenesis window is actually hopeful news: the body is remarkably willing to course-correct when you give it the chance. Great conversation. 🙏🏽 INSTAGRAM education

❤️ INSTAGRAM education
For most people, it’s beneficial. However, in individuals with conditions like PTSD, severe anxiety, or depression, certain types of meditation—especially unguided or intensive practices—can actually amplify distress, trigger intrusive thoughts, or worsen symptoms. The issue isn’t meditation itself, but how and in whom it’s applied. Like any mental practice, it should be approached with context and awareness—not as a one-size-fits-all solution. If people only knew the power of meditation! Glad to see it's getting more attention and people are really embracing it. Thanks for sharing! INSTAGRAM education
One of the fascinating aspects of meditation is how quickly the nervous system responds to it. Even short daily sessions can shift the body from a sympathetic “fight or flight” state toward a parasympathetic “rest and repair” mode.  That shift influences cortisol levels, heart rate variability, and activity in brain regions like the prefrontal cortex and amygdala, which regulate focus and emotional resilience.  In many ways, meditation acts like strength training for attention. A few minutes each day can gradually rewire how the brain handles stress, distraction, and recovery. INSTAGRAM education

I regularly go to a Finnish sauna 1 to 2 times per week. I then go for 30 minutes at 85 degrees, then cool down in open water at 10 degrees for 3 minutes. After that, I immediately go back to the sauna for 30 minutes and then cool down for 1 minute. After a break of about an hour, I often go back for one session of 30 minutes with a short cool-down. In addition, every morning I go into open water (now 10 degrees) for 3 to 5 minutes. In the winter up to 4 degrees but then for 1 to 2 minutes. Really interesting conversation, and thinking of wiring and re-wiring 🧠 one thing I kept thinking about from my lane in facial plastic surgery is that what I often see is actually the inverse of “looksmaxxing.”  A lot of the high-performing men I work with are not trying to use appearance to create a life, identity, or sense of self.  They already built the life. They already have the discipline, the family, the career, the standards, the internal drive.  What starts to happen is that the interface no longer matches the person, especially when it comes to their eyes. This is very rooted in neuroscience.  Their energy, performance, and identity are still going strong. What they start seeing in the mirror just stops reflecting it.  That is a very different psychological category than internet aesthetics, and honestly one that gets missed a lot.  The “one lever fixes everything” mindset is real, especially in men, but the most mature version of this conversation is probably less about trying to become someone else, and more about relieving cognitive dissonance created by the mirror and restoring congruence between internal vitality and external presentation. Very interesting INSTAGRAM education
Attachment, attraction, and relationship formation are complex biopsychosocial processes involving emotional bonding, communication, shared experiences, cultural norms, and individual psychology. While dopamine and attention systems certainly play roles in social interaction, framing phone use during dates primarily as a disruption of specific neural pathways risks oversimplifying how human relationships actually develop. Most evidence suggests that relationship quality is shaped far more by sustained interaction patterns, emotional safety, communication, and shared values than by short-term modulation of neurotransmitter signaling. Neuroscience can help explain aspects of behavior, but translating complex social dynamics directly into “brain pathway” explanations often removes important psychological and social context. The mind is powerful 😍 Keen to listen to this! INSTAGRAM education
What are your thoughts on the idea that exercise acts as a form of hormetic stress—a controlled, beneficial stressor that triggers adaptations to enhance resilience and survival?  During exercise, muscles rapidly use ATP, leading to an accumulation of ADP, which activates AMP-activated protein kinase (AMPK). AMPK plays a key role in the body’s survival response, preparing it to handle potential energy shortages.  When performed at appropriate intensity and duration, exercise can initiate beneficial processes like autophagy, including mitophagy, which recycles damaged mitochondria to promote cellular health and longevity.  However, excessive exercise intensity or prolonged duration can overwhelm the body, triggering apoptosis (programmed cell death) to remove stressed or damaged cells. While apoptosis is necessary for clearing unhealthy cells, excessive activation may overstimulate the immune system, potentially increasing the risk of autoimmune issues.  Adaptation is also critical. Regular intense exercise can lead to metabolic and hemodynamic adjustments, such as a faster metabolic rate, as the body adapts to higher energy demands. Over time, individuals may need longer or more intense sessions to achieve similar benefits. However, there is no universal guideline, as responses vary greatly based on individual physiology.  I welcome your insights on these mechanisms and look forward to your thoughts. Thank you! INSTAGRAM education

The research on menstruating people is shockingly limited. Most of what gets quoted as "science" is actually extrapolation from small studies with tiny sample sizes. So when people make universal claims about what ALL WOMEN should do during their cycle, I get skeptical  I am not supporting the traditional "change your workout every phase" approach. OLD NEWS. What we actually need is INDIVIDUAL ADAPTATION. Not just physically, but neurologically and psychologically too!!!  For example: A woman's progesterone and GABA levels drop significantly in the late luteal phase. Her neurotransmitters are different. nervous system capacity is different. metabolic rate increases by up to 10%. It's FUNDAMENTALLY DIFFERENT from how a man's body operates day-to-day  So why are we treating women's bodies like they should perform THE SAME WAY every single day?  If you push full intensity on a depleted nervous system (those 5-7 days before menstruation), you're not building strength. You're adding stress… youre pushing full gas on an empty tank  I think the message TO THE PUBLIC should be: Your body's individual response is your actual data NOT a generalized study and NOT what worked for someone else INSTAGRAM education

You guys, the comments are getting weird and it’s unnecessary. There’s an important difference between saying men and women “respond similarly to training” and saying their supports and protocols should always be identical. The point isn’t that women need to train exactly like men or fuel the same way. It’s that women are capable of building muscle, producing force, sprinting, and adapting to training across all phases of their cycle.  This matters because a rise in programs that imply women can’t or shouldn’t do certain things at certain times. That’s different from saying if you’re in real pain, exhausted, or run down, you shouldnt modify training. Modifying for pain , inflammation, energy , overall wellness applies to men too. Don’t push through real depletion. But that’s a different conversation than saying women are physiologically UNABLE to make progress because of their cycle.  Some women train straight through all phases and do great. Others need adjustments in volume, intensity, or nutrition. Both can be true. The point isn’t to ignore cycle awareness. It’s to push back on the idea that the basic training response is different or that women can’t get certain results purely because of their cycle. INSTAGRAM education

@hubermanlab @thereadystate I am not sure we can characterize human movement and posture with absolute claims just yet. The industry is very polarized and unwilling to come to a consensus on definitions. Ego, it could be said, is the enemy. In any case, Kelly's comment regarding crisscross applesauce 100-200,000x touches on the idea of ecological dynamics. The problem is that even two-year-olds in contemporary daycare centers in the United States are seated in chairs rather than on the ground. As he alludes to we as any other organism adapt to our environment. According to theory, the S.A.I.D. principle is in effect, and certain mechanical stimuli—or the absence of them—cause the body's tissue (musculoskeletal) to change via Wolf's Law and Davis Law. This helps explain part of the causal chain for how and why we get either a positive effect (“good” mobility) or a negative effect (“poor” mobility). I strongly agree that Movement is medicine and we as a society need to physicaly move more and as well/pain free as we can. Specific or non specific depends on the effect the individual is looking to achieve. My advice is play! Just have fun moving the way you “can” move and explore new patterns that are safe and comfortable for you. INSTAGRAM education

As someone who tracks my cycle and training, I notice the luteal phase often shifts my sleep, heat tolerance, appetite, and perceived exertion. That’s why cycle awareness matters: without it, it’s easy to misread those days as a personal failure, push harder, and end up driving stress higher (sometimes at the expense of recovery and overall hormonal balance). For me, Dr Mindy Pelz (@dr.mindypelz) is a helpful example of a cycle-aware approach, it’s a practical N=1 readiness model. You still train and progress, but you autoregulate based on real feedback from your body, which keeps consistency high and burnout low. Thanks. INSTAGRAM education

Her conclusion seems closest to an individualistic, balanced approach rather than a blanket statement like “skip workouts during your period.” Personally, every other cycle I experience excruciating pain for two days, often to the point of fainting, along with other consequences. Naturally, during those periods I skip workouts. However, there are also cycles where I feel completely fine and continue training as usual. Like many things, this is highly individual. That said, although I admire what this researcher is doing and I am not trying to undermine her work, I often find myself skeptical about studies in such areas in general. One reason I pursued a PhD in mathematics is that, broadly speaking, mathematical results can be proven beyond any doubt. In contrast, many experimental fields establish claims as true beyond reasonable doubt, not beyond all doubt. I am not an expert in her field, and unless I were to study it deeply under an expert and fully understand the details of her research, I will probably remain somewhat cautious when researchers make generalized recommendations about what women should or should not do. However, when a researcher adopts a balanced, individualistic approach (maybe like hers), I feel much more comfortable trusting the conclusion. INSTAGRAM education

Kimberly Snyder sighted this research data about the negative effects of venting. It was a revelation to me, personally. The common belief is that venting will alleviate uncomfortable emotions that feel pent up and somewhat harmful. We believe venting will help us solve or fix the problem. However, it is simply giving more life to the problem. Rather, if we breathe into our hearts, get out of our wild horse mind, center on gratitude and appreciation… we find calm, clarity, and greater intelligence to cope and move forward with optimal discernment. How we deal with uncomfortable emotions is the critical factor in who we become and the impact we have on others. It’s the “wellspring” of life. Addiction, ego-centricity, narcissism, depression, anxiety, nearly every plague of the human mind is a dysfunctional coping posture to combat difficult emotions. INSTAGRAM education

There's a difference between venting "story" (thoughts, ideas, facts) and the deeper emotional layers of the story. The story tends to lead to co-rumination since its all informational. But whats underneath the story is our emotions, values, and needs. When we express from that deepened part of us, we tend to come into a "wider lens" naturally and venting DOES become productive. Its the type of venting that matters. INSTAGRAM education

This hits an important truth many people miss: muscle is built by stimulus quality, not session quantity. Low-frequency, high-intensity training works because it respects recovery biology, allowing motor units, connective tissue, and the nervous system to fully adapt rather than living in a constant half-recovered state. Training to true proximity to failure sends a clean signal for hypertrophy and strength, while the longer recovery window prevents the cortisol and inflammation spiral that derails progress. The genius of this approach is that efficiency isn’t laziness, it’s biological precision. Build hard, recover fully, repeat with intention. INSTAGRAM education

In resident evil most protagonists are immune to the t virus, but not the g virus. This excludes Jill and after raccoon city t was vaccinateable, so no one could get it. But for snake it's even simpler. The types of infections from resident evil including g and even las plagas won't effect anyone after a certain point in the timeline thanks to nanomachines. Every military personnel member in metal gear that would be around at the same time as racoon city wouldn't be able to get infected, and resident evil couldn't happen if metal gear did because biological weapons are not only less reliable than machine warfare, but with nanomachines in most military fighters bloodstreams infections aren't really an effective weapon. Tyrants are a huge threat also, but metal gear far outclassed them. The average T-02 would get easily killed by someone like snake, as snake as taken out guys like Vulcan (twice) and that's just big boss. Naked snake. Solid snake is a whole other matter and he can run as fast as a speeding car and survive microwave bombardment that would kill guys like Captain America. INSTAGRAM comedy
